---
title: "Getting Started with Agent Builder"
description: "Learn the basics of accessing and navigating the Agent Builder"
---

## Introduction

Agent Builder is an agent configuration and lifecycle management tool. It enables organizations to use AI agents to perform specific content-driven tasks or specialize in particular areas of the business, augmenting the human workforce and enabling organization to implement AI at scale.

- Create and manage agentic AIs to automatically execute complex workflows and tasks.
- Specialize created AI agents to better suit their appointed purpose.
- Monitor AI agent performance and diminish potential automation problems for critical tasks while maintaining low human presence for general tasks.

## Accessing The Agent Builder App

Access Agent Builder from the Platform Home Portal.

From the Platform Home, under Applications, click on Agent Builder Studio.

The Agent Builder app is displayed.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

## Accessing The Agent Builder In Studio Modeler

Access Agent Builder from the Studio Modeler.

Note: You can find more information about Studio Modeler and its functionality in the[Hyland Automate Modeler](https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Automate-Modeler/Using) manual.
In the Studio Modeler, in the All Projects menu, choose or create a project where you want to build an Agent.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The Project view will be displayed, where you can create new Agents in the Processes section or edit existing ones in the Agents section.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

## Using Studio Modeler

The Agent Builder application uses Projects as its top-level components. Elements of these projects can be managed using the same actions from the user interface.

The following actions are available:

-  Selecting Language
-  Creating Environment Variables
-  Saving Project Elements
-  Validating Project Elements
-  Choosing an Editor
-  Uploading Project Elements
-  Downloading Project Elements
-  Duplicating Project Elements
-  Deleting Project Elements

## Using Example Projects

To help you get started, example projects are provided. Once you access the example projects you can make copies of them and save them as your own. Once saved, they can be reconfigured for your own purposes, including deploying your own applications that are based on them.

To access and use an example project:

1.  Sign in to Agent Builder.
2.  Select Example Projects from the left pane.
3.  Click Show Details for the example project you want to work with.
4.  Click Duplicate and open.
5.  Enter an appropriate name and click Duplicate.
